channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bumble, Grindr, and Match have agreed to Australia’s online dating code of conduct
item.description: A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday. Bumble, Grindr and Match Group Inc.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Dirty romance novels are shedding their stigma thanks to TikTok
item.description: By now everyone is familiar with #BookTok—but have you stumbled across its spicier cousin #SmutTok? “Smut” refers to books that include one or more sexually explicit scenes and surprisingly (or perhaps unsurprisingly) the erotic category has found a growing fan base on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Hacker made millions from breaking into business Office 365 accounts
item.description: What do you do with the information found in an exec's inbox? One hacker used it to trade stocks and make millions.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google just made a major ChromeOS misstep
item.description: Did you ever watch the early 2000s TV series Scrubs? The show revolved around a young doctor named JD who always meant well but often got lost in his own head and ended up making ill-advised decisions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 12 famous AI disasters
item.description: In 2017, The Economist declared that data, rather than oil, had become the world’s most valuable resource. The refrain has been repeated ever since. Organizations across every industry have been investing, and continue to heavily invest, in data and analytics.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Can digital conversations help disaster-proof your business?
item.description: “The only thing certain in life is uncertainty.” This quote has been attributed to everyone from Pliny the Elder to John F. Kennedy, which means it’s probably been around longer than recorded history and reflects a fundamental truth about how we live. It’s also a fundamental truth about how we work.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Zive, Fast Company

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: All the major legal battles Elon Musk and his companies are facing
item.description: Elon Musk and his companies are embroiled in dozens of lawsuits, ranging from fatal crashes blamed on Tesla Inc.’s Autopilot system to civil rights complaints over the treatment of employees.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Losing Your iPhone: The Risks of Scams and Proactive Steps for Recovery
item.description: Understanding the risks of scams when losing your iPhone, and taking proactive steps to recover or protect your device and data
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The lesson of Helene: you and your business need satellite comms
item.description: Whatever Apple’s long-term plans for satellite connectivity, one facet that cannot be ignored is that its Messages via Satellite system is already saving lives — including among iPhone users affected last week by the horror of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: Revolutionizing Remote Surgery: Groundbreaking Experiment in Switzerland
item.description: Surgeons in Switzerland successfully performed a remote surgery on a pig in Hong Kong using a video game controller, paving the way for future of telesurgery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside the trials and tribulations of a major ransomware attack
item.description: Like many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), ‘JPX Construction’ didn’t see itself as a potential target for a ransomware attack. It wasn’t a high-profile organisation. It didn’t seem to hold much high-value data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JRuby 10 due to arrive in early-2025
item.description: JRuby, which dates back to 2001 as a Ruby language implementation for the JVM, is set to arrive at version 10 shortly after the new year. JRuby 10 promises to be fully compatible with Ruby 3.4.0, a planned update to Ruby that brings changes for frozen string literals and class updates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany’s antitrust authority to look into complaint against Microsoft
item.description: German file hosting cloud service provider Nextcloud appeared to have won a major victory Monday, when the country’s antitrust authority announced it would be initiating what the company described as “the first steps towards an investigation into anti-competitive behavior by Microsoft, including the
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How much are companies willing to spend to get workers back to the office?
item.description: With more and more companies wanting to bring employees back to the office, I pointed out last week the ill-kept secret that there’s a widespread aversion to open office floor plans — or activity-based workplaces, as they have often evolved into today — and that it partially explains why many employ
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, Fox 5
